title: regression building from `git://` URL
body:
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------


**Description**

`podman build` regressed in building from `git://`

For a while now I've got a build that runs that builds from a `git://` url. This started failing earlier this year,  but I didn't realize it until recently. Here is how it fails today:


```
[runner@dustymabe ~]$ podman build --pull-always -t localhost/dustymabecom:latest git://github.com/dustymabe/dustymabe.com#main                              
Error: error prepping temporary context directory: cloning "git://github.com/dustymabe/dustymabe.com#main" to "/var/tmp/buildah3856140366":                  
Cloning into '/var/tmp/buildah3856140366'...                                                                                                                 
fatal: unable to connect to github.com:                                                                                                                      
github.com[0: 140.82.112.4]: errno=Connection timed out                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                             
: exit status 128
```

On this node this worked up until March. Inspecting the last container image that was built successfully using this model I see:

```
[runner@dustymabe ~]$ podman inspect cc76ed6eef3e | jq ".[].Created"
"2022-03-09T23:14:46.382232815Z"
[runner@dustymabe ~]$ podman inspect cc76ed6eef3e | jq ".[].Labels"
{
  "io.buildah.version": "1.23.1",
  "license": "MIT",
  "name": "fedora",
  "vendor": "Fedora Project",
  "version": "35"
}
```

So this worked up until March of this year with Buildah `1.23.1`. So I started to go back to older versions of Fedora CoreOS to try to find where this broke. What I found is that it now doesn't work even on the older versions. My only explanation for this is that something changed on the remote server (GitHub). Still I think it's worth reporting here so we can try to get to the bottom of the issue.

Workaround: I worked around this issue by switching the URL to this form: `https://github.com/dustymabe/dustymabe.com.git#main`



**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. run `podman build -t localhost/dustymabecom:latest git://github.com/dustymabe/dustymabe.com#main`



**Describe the results you received:**

Failed build `unable to connect to github.com`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Successful build


**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
podman-4.1.1-1.fc36.aarch64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
$ podman info | grep -i buildah
  buildahVersion: 1.26.1
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.1
API Version:  4.1.1
Go Version:   go1.18.3
Built:        Wed Jun 15 14:33:05 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
$ cat /etc/*release
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="36.20220703.1.1 (CoreOS)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=36
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f36"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora CoreOS 36.20220703.1.1"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:36"
HOME_URL="https://getfedora.org/coreos/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=36
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=36
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Legal:PrivacyPolicy"
VARIANT="CoreOS"
VARIANT_ID=coreos
OSTREE_VERSION='36.20220703.1.1'
DEFAULT_HOSTNAME=localhost
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
Fedora release 36 (Thirty Six)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
$ uname -a
Linux dustymabe.com 5.18.7-200.fc36.aarch64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat Jun 25 19:48:53 UTC 2022 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
$ ls -l /etc/containers/storage.conf /var/home/$USER/.config/containers/storage.conf
ls: cannot access '/etc/containers/storage.conf': No such file or directory
ls: cannot access '/var/home/runner/.config/containers/storage.conf': No such file or directory
```

comments.body: This is a [github policy decision](https://github.blog/2021-09-01-improving-git-protocol-security-github/) (search in-page for "`unenc`"). Not much we can do about it, sorry.

Thanks @edsantiago. That's good information. 

Can we get some of [the documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4d111fa9af0352d25d4c9ecc7d203f20f5727d09/docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md#building-an-image-using-a-git-repository) updated to not use an example that doesn't work?

Maybe we could also enhance it to mention the above information about github being a problem there.
Though even better, we could just detect `git://github.com` and tell the user why it won't work with the link to the decision you gave me above.

Can we update the `podman-build` docs too?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/7af523ea5af8ba8ad61cd4b81a1494a42ac7b479/docs/source/markdown/podman-build.1.md.in#L991
Also related to #4032. Using `git://` should issue a warning, it is insecure and users probably want either `https://` for public repos ro `ssh://` for private repos.
